message.content:
Based on the screenshot provided, here's what I can observe about the contact section in dark mode:

The screenshot shows a dark-themed website with a CRT/terminal glitch effect visible in the background. The effect appears as orange/golden glowing lines or waves that create a digital, retro-futuristic aesthetic. The text "Shannon Rodriguez" is prominently displayed with "Shannon" in white and "Rodriguez" in an orange/amber color that matches the background effect.

Regarding your specific questions:

1. Yes, the CRT/terminal glitch effect is visible behind the text, creating an orange/golden wave pattern that serves as the background.

2. The text appears to be quite readable - the white "Shannon" contrasts well against the dark background, and the orange "Rodriguez" complements the background effect while still being distinguishable.

3. There doesn't appear to be any black overlay issues that would hinder readability. The design maintains good contrast between text and background.

The overall aesthetic is a modern dark mode design with a tech-inspired background that doesn't compromise text readability. The color scheme effectively uses orange/amber tones both in the text and background effect to create visual harmony.
